modelConfig.title: Configuration
modelConfig.noModel: No Forecast model is installed or configured.

horizon_max_override.label: Horizon limit
horizon_max_override.description: Maximum number of future points this model may forecast in one call. Example: daily data with 31 means 31 days; hourly data with 24 means 24 hours. Empty or 0 keeps the native model limit.

context_length.label: History length
context_length.description: Number of past points given to the model to learn trend, seasonality and breaks. Higher uses more history, but can slow down and dilute recent changes.

quantiles.label: Quantiles
quantiles.description: Probabilistic levels returned by the model. 0.5 is the central forecast; 0.1 and 0.9 form the lower/upper uncertainty range. A wider range means higher uncertainty.

precision.label: Precision
precision.description: Numeric format used locally. auto lets the engine choose; float32 favors stability; float16/bfloat16 save RAM/VRAM when supported.

dtype.label: Numeric type
dtype.description: Numeric type used during inference. auto is recommended; float32 is safer; float16/bfloat16 can be faster and lighter but are not supported everywhere.

batch_size.label: Batch size
batch_size.description: Number of series processed together. Increasing can speed up large datasets but uses more RAM/VRAM; lower it if the model runs out of memory.

horizon_len.label: Internal horizon
horizon_len.description: Internal output length for engines that separate requested horizon from computed horizon. Keep default unless long horizons are refused or under-produced.

prediction_length.label: Prediction length
prediction_length.description: Number of future steps generated by engines exposing this setting. It must match the requested horizon: too low cuts the forecast, too high wastes compute.

confidence_level.label: Confidence
confidence_level.description: Requested uncertainty interval width. 0.9 means 90%: wider and more conservative; 0.8 is tighter but less cautious.

level.label: Level
level.description: Interval level sent to TimeGPT. Example: 90 requests a 90% confidence range. Higher levels display wider ranges.

normalization.label: Normalization
normalization.description: Stabilizes the scale of series before forecasting. Useful when values vary by large orders of magnitude; disable only when raw scale is meaningful signal.

quantile_head.label: Quantile head
quantile_head.description: Enables the native probabilistic head when the model has one. Use it when you need a real uncertainty range, not only a central line.

xreg_mode.label: XReg
xreg_mode.description: Controls how external variables such as weather, traffic or holidays are used. auto lets the engine decide; disabling returns closer to target-only forecasting.

clean_ex_first.label: Clean exogenous
clean_ex_first.description: Asks TimeGPT to clean external variables before inference. Useful when covariates have gaps or outliers, but adds latency.

finetune_steps.label: Finetune steps
finetune_steps.description: Small adaptation steps on the current dataset. More steps can fit history better, but take longer and may overfit short histories.

finetune_loss.label: Finetune loss
finetune_loss.description: Optimization objective used during TimeGPT adaptation. Keep default unless you know how you want errors penalized.

finetune_depth.label: Finetune depth
finetune_depth.description: Strength of TimeGPT adaptation. Low is light tuning; high adapts more strongly but is riskier on small histories.

feature_contributions.label: Contributions
feature_contributions.description: Requests estimated impact of external variables. Useful to explain a forecast, but can slow calls or be unavailable depending on the service.

decode_block_size.label: Decode block
decode_block_size.description: Number of future points generated per Toto block. Larger can speed long horizons but uses more memory; smaller is safer on limited machines.

has_missing_values.label: Missing values
has_missing_values.description: Tells the engine that history contains missing values. Enable only when the dataset has gaps; otherwise leave it off to avoid unnecessary handling.

non_negative_output.label: Positive output
non_negative_output.description: Prevents negative forecasts or lower bounds. Enable for sales, revenue, volume, stock or any metric that cannot go below zero.

target_dim.label: Target dimension
target_dim.description: Number of target dimensions predicted together by MOIRAI. 1 means one target; increase only for real multi-dimensional datasets.

feat_dynamic_real_dim.label: Future variables
feat_dynamic_real_dim.description: Number of known future numeric variables given to MOIRAI, such as planned weather, calendar or promotions. Must match the actual future columns.

past_feat_dynamic_real_dim.label: Past variables
past_feat_dynamic_real_dim.description: Number of historical numeric variables given to MOIRAI. Explains the past; it does not replace known future variables.

scale_factor.label: Scale factor
scale_factor.description: Adjusts FlowState internal amplitude. Keep default unless outputs are systematically too low or too high.

batch_first.label: Batch first
batch_first.description: Dimension order expected by some PyTorch engines. Change only if the model reports a shape/order error.

known_future_covariates.label: Future covariates
known_future_covariates.description: Allows using variables already known in the future: holidays, planned prices, weather forecasts or campaigns. Enable when these columns are filled in the future zone.

local_mode.label: Local mode
local_mode.description: Forces local execution when the engine supports several modes. Keep enabled to avoid external calls and keep data on the machine.

probabilistic_output.label: Probabilistic output
probabilistic_output.description: Requests uncertainty bounds or multiple paths instead of one central value. Useful for risk, but can take longer.

backend.label: Backend
backend.description: Internal backend used by the adapter. auto picks the best available; change mainly for diagnostics or to force CPU/GPU support.

output_type.label: Output type
output_type.description: Chooses central forecast or forecast with quantiles/uncertainty. Use quantiles when you want a confidence range.

frequency_resampling.label: Resampling
frequency_resampling.description: Controls whether dates are regularized before forecasting. Useful when dates are missing; risky when gaps are meaningful.

preserve_positivity.label: Preserve positivity
preserve_positivity.description: Asks Kairos to keep outputs positive. Enable for metrics that cannot be negative, such as revenue, orders, traffic or volume.

average_with_flipped_input.label: Flipped input
average_with_flipped_input.description: Runs Kairos on a reversed input too and averages results. Can stabilize the curve, but adds compute time.

generation.label: Generation
generation.description: Enables the engine generative mode when available. Useful for richer trajectories; keep default for classic forecasting.

num_samples.label: Samples
num_samples.description: Number of simulated paths used to estimate quantiles. Higher is more stable but slower.

device.label: Device
device.description: Default hardware inherited from Settings > Advanced. auto lets the app choose; GPU accelerates if available; CPU is slower but more compatible.

model_unload_timeout.label: Unload timeout
model_unload_timeout.description: How long the model stays loaded after use, inherited from Settings > Advanced. Longer avoids reloads; shorter frees memory sooner.

models.title: Forecast models
models.noneAvailable: No model is available for this launch.

descriptions.chronos-bolt-tiny: Very light model for first tests and small machines.
descriptions.chronos-bolt-mini: Balanced local option for speed and hardware cost.
descriptions.chronos-bolt-small: Recommended local choice for more serious forecasts.
descriptions.chronos-bolt-base: Heavier local version for more demanding workloads.
descriptions.chronos-2: Newer local Chronos engine, ready for future evolution toward richer context support.
descriptions.timegpt-2-mini: Simple cloud option for quick tests with external context.
descriptions.timegpt-2-standard: Balanced cloud option for richer series and business context.
descriptions.timegpt-2-pro: Advanced cloud option for more ambitious multi-series use.
